raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"Central causal claims — that panel diversity causes enrichment, that median Z-score is a calibrated bioactivity proxy — require checking premise-to-conclusion coherence and whether the ablation logic actually isolates the diversity effect.","R1.3":"The paper's load-bearing empirical numbers (2.9–3.4× enrichment, ~30% ablation drop) must be traceable to the stated experimental design; misalignment between those figures and what the described protocol can actually establish is the primary failure mode.","R1.4":"MolPanel is explicitly positioned as a general, novel contribution; assessing whether median-aggregated panel consensus over heterogeneous property predictors represents a genuine delta over existing ensemble virtual-screening and multi-predictor consensus methods is plausibly the user's core interest."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Are you primarily asking whether the empirical claims (enrichment ratios, ablation results) are adequately supported by the described experimental evidence, or whether MolPanel constitutes a sufficiently novel contribution relative to existing ensemble and multi-predictor consensus approaches in virtual screening?"}

rationale.R1.2: Central causal claims — that panel diversity causes enrichment, that median Z-score is a calibrated bioactivity proxy — require checking premise-to-conclusion coherence and whether the ablation logic actually isolates the diversity effect.
rationale.R1.3: The paper's load-bearing empirical numbers (2.9–3.4× enrichment, ~30% ablation drop) must be traceable to the stated experimental design; misalignment between those figures and what the described protocol can actually establish is the primary failure mode.
rationale.R1.4: MolPanel is explicitly positioned as a general, novel contribution; assessing whether median-aggregated panel consensus over heterogeneous property predictors represents a genuine delta over existing ensemble virtual-screening and multi-predictor consensus methods is plausibly the user's core interest.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you primarily asking whether the empirical claims (enrichment ratios, ablation results) are adequately supported by the described experimental evidence, or whether MolPanel constitutes a sufficiently novel contribution relative to existing ensemble and multi-predictor consensus approaches in virtual screening?
